info.title: InvoiceShelf API
info.description: REST API for InvoiceShelf — open-source invoicing & expense tracking. Authenticate with a Sanctum personal access token (Authorization: Bearer <token>). Company-scoped endpoints additionally require a `company` header carrying the company ID.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: ID of the company the request operates on (multi-tenancy).

post.summary: Handle the incoming request

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: The company slug

get.summary: Return the current AI config defaults plus the driver list for the wizard form

post.summary: Persist the installer's AI config choice and advance the wizard step

get.summary: Return the AI driver list for the admin UI — same shape as the exchange rate endpoint

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: ID of the company the request operates on (multi-tenancy).

get.summary: Get the global AI configuration with decrypted API key masked for response

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: ID of the company the request operates on (multi-tenancy).

post.description:
If the submitted api_key is the masked placeholder, we retain the stored value —
otherwise the user would have to re-enter the key every time they save the form.
post.summary: Persist the global AI configuration

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: ID of the company the request operates on (multi-tenancy).

post.summary: Test the currently configured AI provider by instantiating its driver and calling validateConnection()

post.description:
If `conversation_id` is omitted (or belongs to a conversation the user
doesn't own), we start a fresh conversation scoped to the current
(company_id, user_id). This matches the "new chat" UX where the user
opens the drawer and starts typing immediately.
post.summary: Send a message into a conversation and get the assistant's reply

and the driver fields are discarded (same pattern as the mail company override).
